data.title: Vancouver: Bitcoin reserve proposal faces pushback from city staff due to charter rules. Mayor's plan at risk ahead of Tuesday council vote.
data.description: Officials in Vancouver have confirmed that according to the Vancouver Charter, the use of Bitcoin is not allowed within the city. They are suggesting to discard Mayor Ken Sim's 2024 reserve plan prior to the upcoming council vote scheduled for Tuesday.

data.title: Officials in Vancouver resist Mayor Ken Sim's Bitcoin reserve proposal; city staff cites Vancouver Charter, advises against it before council vote.
data.description: Officials in Vancouver have clarified that according to the Vancouver Charter, the use of Bitcoin is not allowed. They have suggested that Mayor Ken Sim's proposal to create a reserve for 2024 should be abandoned before the council's vote scheduled for Tuesday.

data.title: After $3.9M losses due to December 2025 breach, FLOW rebounds with 16% increase as fix deployed.
data.description: Flow Network successfully addressed an exploit from December 2025 which led to the production of fake tokens, resulting in losses amounting to $3.9 million. Following the implementation of a solution by the protocol, the FLOW token experienced a 16% increase in value. For additional information, refer to the complete article.

data.title: Vancouver Bitcoin reserve plan facing opposition from city officials; they advise against Mayor Ken Sim's proposal before March 10 vote.
data.description: According to city officials in Vancouver, Bitcoin does not comply with the Vancouver Charter, leading to a recommendation to reject Mayor Ken Sim's 2024 reserve proposal. The proposal is slated for closure prior to a council vote scheduled for March 10.

data.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Genesco Expectations Ahead Of Q4 Earnings
data.description: Genesco Inc. (NYSE: GCO) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on March 6 before the market opens. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $3.58 per share for the Nashville-based company. The consensus prediction for Genesco's quarterly revenue stands at $790.53 million, an increase from last year's $745.95 million. Genesco hiked its FY26 adjusted EPS guidance on Jan. 12. The company's shares saw a 1.1% decline, closing at $26.09 on Thursday. For more details on analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.

data.title: Intended Retirement of Independent Non-executive Director and changes of composition of board committees
data.description: HUTCHMED (China) Limited announced that Professor Mok Shu Kam, Tony, an Independent Non-executive Director of the company, will not seek re-election after serving for over eight years. As per Hong Kong Listing Rules, which impose a nine-year cap on the tenure of independent non-executive directors, Professor Mok will retire from the Board at the upcoming annual general meeting on May 12, 2026. Following this, he will also relinquish his positions as chairman and member of the board committees.
